# Normalize function
import re
import unicodedata
def normalize_ar(s):
    # remove tatweel
    s = s.replace('\u0640','')
    # remove diacritics
    s = re.sub(r'[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u06D6-\u06ED\u0670\u06E0-\u06EF]','',s)
    # remove punctuation
    s = re.sub(r'[^\w\s\p{Arabic}]', '', s)
    # Above regex might not support \p{Arabic} in python; instead remove common punctuation
    s = re.sub(r'[.,،؟!؛:"\'\(\)\[\]{}«»\-—…·•·]', '', s)
    # unify hamza forms
    s = s.replace('أ','ا').replace('إ','ا').replace('آ','ا').replace('ؤ','و').replace('ئ','ي').replace('ى','ي')
    # collapse spaces
    s = re.sub(r'\s+',' ', s).strip()
    return s

# But previous regex used \p{Arabic} invalid. Let's write a simpler one: 
def normalize_ar2(s):
    # remove tatweel
    s = s.replace('\u0640','')
    # remove diacritics (harakat)
    s = re.sub(r'[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u06D6-\u06ED\u0670\u06E0-\u06EF]', '', s)
    # remove punctuation characters common
    s = re.sub(r'[^\u0600-\u06FF\u0750-\u077F\u08A0-\u08FF\s]', '', s)
    # unify hamza
    s = s.replace('أ','ا').replace('إ','ا').replace('آ','ا').replace('ؤ','و').replace('ئ','ي').replace('ى','ي')
    # collapse spaces
    s = re.sub(r'\s+',' ', s).strip()
    return s

# Test normalization on the substring and snippet
substring = "وَعَدَدَ لَكُمْ فِعْلَ الشُكْرِ"
snippet = "وَعَدَدَ ل...َ الشُكْرِ"
print(normalize_ar2(substring))
print(normalize_ar2(snippet))

target_norm = normalize_ar2(substring)
matches = []
for entry in quran:
    ayah = entry.get('ayah_text','')
    ayah_norm = normalize_ar2(ayah)
    if target_norm in ayah_norm:
        matches.append((entry['surah_id'], entry['ayah_id'], entry['surah_name'], ayah, ayah_norm))
len(matches)

from difflib import SequenceMatcher
def sim(a,b):
    return SequenceMatcher(None,a,b).ratio()

scores = []
for entry in quran:
    ayah = entry.get('ayah_text','')
    ayah_norm = normalize_ar2(ayah)
    sc = sim(target_norm, ayah_norm)
    scores.append((sc, entry['surah_id'], entry['ayah_id'], entry['surah_name'], ayah, ayah_norm))
# sort descending
scores_sorted = sorted(scores, key=lambda x: -x[0])
scores_sorted[:20]

# Inspect hadiths entries
for i in range(0,5):
    print(i, list(hadiths[i].keys()))
    # print some text
    print(hadiths[i].get('hadithTxt') or hadiths[i].get('Matn')[:200])
    print()

# search hadiths for normalized target
target_norm = normalize_ar2(substring)
hadith_matches = []
for entry in hadiths:
    txt = entry.get('hadithTxt') or entry.get('Matn') or ''
    txt_norm = normalize_ar2(txt)
    if target_norm in txt_norm:
        hadith_matches.append((entry['hadithID'], entry.get('title',''), txt[:200]))
len(hadith_matches)

# compute top 20 hadiths by similarity
scores_h = []
for entry in hadiths:
    txt = entry.get('hadithTxt') or entry.get('Matn') or ''
    txt_norm = normalize_ar2(txt)
    sc = SequenceMatcher(None, target_norm, txt_norm).ratio()
    scores_h.append((sc, entry['hadithID'], entry.get('title',''), txt[:200]))
scores_h_sorted = sorted(scores_h, key=lambda x: -x[0])
scores_h_sorted[:20]

# Search for 'الشكر' in ayah texts normalized
target_word = normalize_ar2("الشُكْرِ")
res = []
for entry in quran:
    ayah_norm = normalize_ar2(entry['ayah_text'])
    if 'الشكر' in ayah_norm:
        res.append((entry['surah_id'], entry['ayah_id'], entry['surah_name'], entry['ayah_text']))
len(res), res[:20]
